informal_statement.text: Given equal-length slices of non-negative item weights and item values, and a non-negative integer capacity, return the maximum total value achievable by choosing a subset whose total weight does not exceed the capacity. Each item may be selected at most once.
preconditions: weights and values have the same length
every weight is a non-negative integer
every value is a non-negative integer
required_properties: the result is witnessed by some subset S of item indices whose total weight <= capacity and total value equals the result
no subset whose total weight <= capacity has a strictly greater total value
each item index is used at most once in any witness subset
edge_cases: capacity 0 (result is 0 unless there is a zero-weight positive-value item)
a single item with weight > capacity
multiple items of identical weight and value

semantic_units.description: Alignment precondition: weights and values have the same length.
common_failure_modes: allowing trailing values to be ignored

semantic_units.description: Capacity feasibility: the witness subset has total weight at most the capacity.
Value witness: there is a valid selection whose total value equals the returned result.
common_failure_modes: stating only a value upper bound

semantic_units.description: Optimality: no valid selection with total weight <= capacity achieves a strictly greater total value.
0/1 uniqueness: items appear at most once in any witness selection.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ws : Weights) (vs : Values) (cap : Nat), ws.length = vs.length → ∃ (sel : Selection), ValidSelection ws sel ∧ totalWeight ws sel ≤ cap ∧ totalValue vs sel = knapsack01 ws vs cap
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values are length-aligned, there exists a valid feasible selection whose total value equals the returned result.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ws : Weights) (vs : Values) (cap : Nat) (sel : Selection), ws.length = vs.length → ValidSelection ws sel → totalWeight ws sel ≤ cap → totalValue vs sel ≤ knapsack01 ws vs cap
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values are length-aligned, no valid feasible selection beats the returned result.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation asserts that `weights.len() == values.len()`. Any correctness statement assumes this alignment.
Define the intended result as the maximum total value among all subsets of items whose total weight is ≤ capacity.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
let cap := capacity.toNat
let dp0 : Array Nat := Array.mk (cap+1) 0
-- Invariant after processing first i items:
-- for all c ≤ cap, dp[c] equals the optimum value using items {0,...,i-1} with total weight ≤ c.
∀ (i : Nat) (c : Nat), c ≤ cap →
  dp_i[c] =
    (Finset.sup (by
      -- supremum over subsets of {0,...,i-1} with weight ≤ c
      simp))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the core DP invariant: after processing i items, dp[c] stores the maximum value achievable with total weight ≤ c using only the first i items.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
Assuming the invariant holds for i items, let w := weights[i], v := values[i], and w ≤ cap.
Then for all c ≤ cap:
  dp_{i+1}[c] =
    if c < w then dp_i[c]
    else max (dp_i[c]) (dp_i[c - w] + v)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the update rule matches 0/1 knapsack: either do not take item i (value dp_i[c]) or take it (value dp_i[c-w]+v), and choose the maximum. For c<w, taking is impossible.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
For the inner loop iterating c in decreasing order from w to cap, the update to dp[c] uses dp[c-w] from the previous outer iteration (i.e., before item i is considered), not a value updated earlier in the same outer iteration.
Formally: during processing item i, for any c with w ≤ c ≤ cap, the value read at dp[c-w] equals dp_i[c-w].
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Justify that iterating `for c in (w..=cap).rev()` enforces the 0/1 constraint (each item used at most once) when using a 1-D rolling array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
If w > cap then the outer iteration leaves dp unchanged, and the invariant for i+1 items follows from the invariant for i items.
∀ c ≤ cap, dp_{i+1}[c] = dp_i[c].
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: When an item’s weight exceeds capacity, the code continues without updating dp; correctness must reflect that such an item cannot be part of any feasible subset.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: Before processing any items (i=0), for all c ≤ cap, dp_0[c] = 0, which equals the optimum value using the empty subset with weight ≤ c.
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The dp array is initialized to zeros, representing that with no items the best achievable value is 0 for any capacity.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
After processing all n = weights.length items, dp_n[cap] equals the optimum value among all subsets of items with total weight ≤ cap.
Therefore knapsack_01 weights values capacity = dp_n[cap] = knapsack01_spec weights values capacity.
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Conclude that the returned value dp[capacity] matches the specification: the maximum total value over all subsets whose total weight does not exceed capacity.
